title: cb-norm of Phi^2-Phi (eta-defect) via Watrous diamond-norm SDP + MOSEK golden master
description: The eta-idempotence parameter ||Phi^2-Phi||_cb (tex:347-354) is the project's central quantity. Phi^2-Phi is Hermitian-preserving but NOT CP, so the CP closed form ||Phi||_cb=||Phi(I)|| does NOT apply -- a full SDP is required (confirmed by web research; both naive 'Choi operator norm' formulas are wrong). Canonical route: Watrous 2012 SDP (arXiv:1207.5726) on the Choi matrix of Phi^2-Phi, dim n^2 x n^2. Double path: MOSEK (C API or via cvxpy/Julia Convex.jl) -- also the GOLDEN MASTER oracle. Certified arb path: NO arb-native SDP solver exists; credible route is solve-in-double then rational-certificate / verified-SDP post-processing (KKT + duality-gap check in FLINT/arb). Split out from aic-c7n because it is heavy, partially independent of the representation core, and needs solver infra.
design: Ampliation truncation is RIGOROUS: sup over k achieved at k=dim(input) (Watrous 2012; Gutoski-Watrous arXiv:1008.4636) -- this RESOLVES MODULE_PLAN escalation #2 (cb-norm truncation N); N=dim suffices, no conjecture. Choi matrix of Phi^2-Phi = C_{Phi^2}-C_Phi (linearity); Phi^2 has Kraus {K_a K_b}. Refs: Watrous 2009 arXiv:0901.4709, 2012 arXiv:1207.5726; Johnston-Kribs-Paulsen arXiv:0711.3636; QETLAB DiamondNorm/CBNorm; SchattenNorms.jl. Certified-arb prototype pattern: arXiv:2512.17713 (treat as technique, not load-bearing citation). Audition double-path candidates: MOSEK direct vs cvxpy; vs power-iteration lower-bound estimator (Candidate D, fast but uncertified).
acceptance_criteria: Double path computes ||Phi^2-Phi||_cb matching MOSEK golden master to solver tol on a corpus incl. eta=0 oracle (defect=0), known qubit channels, adversarial near-idempotent (eta=1e-3,1e-8). cb-norm of any UCP map certified =1. Certified arb ball brackets the double value at the eta->0 boundary OR fails loud if precision insufficient.
notes: SCOPING VERIFIED (2026-05-28, Julia+MOSEK probe, 8 tests OPTIMAL). ARCH=Route B: C core computes Choi(Phi^2-Phi) (both paths, cross-checked); diamond-norm SDP in JULIA (Convex.jl 0.16 + MosekTools + Mosek.jl 11.2), NO Python. KEY: Convention-A Choi has trace=n for UCP, Watrous SDP is trace-1 calibrated => ||Lambda||_diamond = (2/n)*SDP_optval (verified n=1..4). SDP (Watrous 2012): vars X(complex n^2xn^2), P,Q(HermPSD n^2); maximize Re tr(J' X) s.t. [[P,X],[X',Q]]>=0, P+Q=I_{n^2}; Convex.jl handles complex natively (use isposdef(x), silent=). ANALYTIC ANCHORS (re-derived, correct): ||Dep_2-id_2||_d=2(1-1/d^2)=1.5; Phi_t^2-Phi_t = t(1-t)(Dep-id) so ||.||_d=t(1-t)*1.5 (0.315@.3, .01485@.01). First increment: C aic_ucp_compose (Kraus of PhioPsi={L_b K_a}) + aic_ucp_choi_diff (both paths); tools/gen_fixtures_d24.jl (committed julia/env Project+Manifest) -> tests/fixtures_d24.inc.h (generated C arrays); test reads fixtures, checks Choi substrate + eta=0 oracle. DEFERRED: certified-arb cb-ball (KKT/rational-certificate, no arb SDP solver) -> escalation; the C-callable SDP via ccall -> Julia pkg E5 (aic-obc).
close_reason: Increment 1 SHIPPED + triple-verified. C core: aic_ucp_compose (Kraus of PhioPsi={L_b K_a}, Heisenberg) + aic_ucp_choi_diff (Choi(Phi^2-Phi)), both number paths, double-vs-arb@53 cross-checked, eta=0 Choi oracle. Julia+MOSEK golden master (tools/gen_fixtures_d24.jl, committed julia/env): the (2/n)-normalized Watrous SDP computes ||Phi^2-Phi||_diamond, validated against analytic anchors across n=2,3,4 (Dep_d-id_d=2(1-1/d^2): 1.5,16/9,15/8; Phi_t family t(1-t)*that) AND the paper's own example eta*sqrt(1-eta) (tex:367-378). 17-fixture corpus incl. complex_qubit (guards the Choi-conjugation convention; teeth-proven) + rank-deficient block_lowrank3. C-vs-Julia Choi cross-check max-diff 2.7e-15; test_ucp_d24 n=71; zero warnings. Normalization triple-verified (hand-derivation + Julia regen + independent CLARABEL re-derivation). REMAINING -> aic-m24: certified-arb cb-ball (no arb SDP solver, research escalation) + a reusable callable value entry point (E5 ccall). The eta VALUE is computable now via the Julia golden master, unblocking assoc_ecsa (aic-92f).

title: Certified eig for DEGENERATE Hermitian spectra (projections, C* multiplicities)
description: T-mat found FLINT 3.0.1 has NO Hermitian eigensolver and its certified path acb_mat_eig_simple REQUIRES a simple (non-degenerate) spectrum (returns 0 -> we abort on repeated eigenvalues). But this project is dominated by degenerate spectra: projections have eigenvalues 0/1 with high multiplicity; the target algebras are direct sums of matrix blocks (B(L_j)) with multiplicities. So certified eigendecomposition of degenerate Hermitian matrices is foundational and currently missing. Audition (Law 4) certified routes: acb_mat_eig_multiple / acb_mat_eig_multiple_rump (cluster/invariant-subspace enclosures); spectral-gap clustering then per-cluster enclosure; or projector-based deflation. Must handle exact 0/1 multiplicities. Cross-check vs simple-spectrum path on non-degenerate inputs; eta=0 / exact-projection oracle.
notes: AUDITION CANDIDATE for certified extraction (review of aic-c7n, 2026-05-28): besides the eig route (acb_mat_eig_multiple_rump / global_enclosure), audition an EIG-FREE bipartite/blocked Cholesky of the PSD Choi matrix -> Kraus ops (standard LA; arb-friendly, sidesteps the simple-spectrum wall entirely). The ucp module shipped only the LAPACK-double eig extraction (aic_ucp_choi_to_kraus_latd); the certified arb extraction + certified carrier RANK (gap-dependent) remain blocked here. Compare candidates on: certifiability, robustness on rank-deficient/maximally-degenerate Choi, accuracy@prec, wall time.

description: Core data structures (shard G): Choi matrix C_Phi, isometry V (Phi(X)=V^dag(X tensor 1_F)V), Kraus K_a. Convention A: C_Phi=sum E_pq(x)Phi(E_pq), K-first/H-second; unital <=> Tr_K(C_Phi)=I_H (partial_trace_left). SCOPE OF THIS BEAD: data model + Kraus<->Choi<->Stinespring conversions + CP check (Cholesky/zpotrf eig-free double; acb_mat_eig_global_enclosure arb) + unital check + carrier (Q=sum K_a K_a^dag) + cb-norm CLOSED FORM for CP/UCP (||Phi(I)||, =1 for UCP). DEFERRED to aic-d24: ||Phi^2-Phi||_cb (SDP). Certified Kraus EXTRACTION (eig route) blocked on aic-w4o.1; double path (zheevd) handles degeneracy now. title: v0.2: rigorous two-sided diamond-norm cb cert + F4.2 dim-independence canary (Convex.jl dual stalls n>=6)
description: DEFERRED F4.2 follow-up to the th_factorization headline (aic-tff, closed on F4.1). The DUAL (diamond_norm_dual, min over opnorm-of-partial-trace epigraphs) STALLS at SLOW_PROGRESS for n>=6 in Convex.jl (returns a loose 0.0237 it cannot drive to the primal optimum; tight 1e-14 tol -> ~100s/~20GB, n=7 OOMs at 47GB; relaxed 1e-9 -> still SLOW_PROGRESS). title: Pipeline must fail-loud (not HANG) when input violates eta<1/4 / rho(Phi^2-Phi)<1/4
description: Rule-4 gap exposed 2026-05-31 (a hostile-review eta-sweep probe hung a whole session). Feeding the almost-idempotent pipeline a channel with rho(Phi^2-Phi) >= 1/4 (e.g. make_mixconj* with t~0.45, eta past 1/4 — outside the th_factorization hypothesis) makes funcalc (theta(2Phi-1) / global sgn, aic-8hz) and/or aic_cstar_build spin without converging and WITHOUT a fail-loud abort: it hangs. The documented basin is .tex:520 (||X^2 - x0^2 I|| < x0^2; here rho(Phi^2-Phi)<1/4). aic_assoc_regularize / aic_prop_P / aic_cstar_build should ASSERT the precondition up front (or cap iterations and abort with a clear message) so an out-of-regime input fails FAST (Rule 4) instead of hanging. See CLAUDE.md "Probe/sweep hygiene". Relates to aic-8hz / aic-68c (near-eta=1/4 convergence).
notes: Research (aic-95g.1 session, Explore leg): ROOT CAUSE = NO precondition check at the aic_assoc_regularize PUBLIC entry (src/aic_assoc_regularize.c). The rho(Phi^2-Phi)<1/4 / ||X^2-I||<1 basin guard is buried DEEP in aic_prop_P (src/aic_funcalc_proj.c) + aic_sgn (funcalc_sgn.c/funcalc_global.c). Most iteration loops already hardcap+abort (Newton-Schulz/Denman-Beavers/global-Newton/xpow: k<100/200 -> fprintf+abort, fail-loud OK). The Gelfand certifier aic_funcalc_int_gelfand_rho (funcalc_global.c ~L85-111, k_max=32) returns 0 SILENTLY; callers (prop_P, sgn_newton_global) check it + abort. The 'hang' is NOT infinite: out-of-basin the 32-power Gelfand loop runs with arb RADIUS INFLATION -> each acb_mat_mul/arb_root_ui slow -> minutes/build, looks hung in a sweep. FIX (Phase1, ~40 LOC): certify rho(Phi^2-Phi)<1/4 at aic_assoc_regularize entry, BEFORE the O(n^4) superop build -> abort loud at the API boundary. MUST verify the exact Gelfand API signature + regularize structure before coding (Explore guessed line numbers). ALSO check the cstar_build master loop (Stage-1 greedy projection/extension) for a truly-unbounded loop. TEST GAP: no executable fail-loud test (abort crashes the binary) -> add a SUBPROCESS-based test (fork + assert nonzero exit + stderr match); reusable for other Rule-4 guards.
close_reason: Fail-loud entry guard added to aic_assoc_regularize (certifies spectral rho(Phi^2-Phi)<1/4 via eig-free Gelfand, aborts with a clear .tex-cited channel-level message out-of-regime). REFRAMING (hostile-verified): the pipeline never actually hung — prop_P's Gelfand guard already aborts ~0.2s (old hang was pre-aic-8hz op-norm). make_mixconj CANNOT go out-of-regime at any t (FINDINGS C15); genuine out-of-regime = reflection conjugation diag(1,-1). Deliverables: the project's FIRST executable fail-loud test (reusable fork+SIGALRM watchdog subprocess harness; NEG Z-conj aborts+names basin, POS incl aic-8hz spectral-relaxation must NOT be rejected; mutation-proven; fast 0.22s) + the entry-guard message + sign-label Law-1 cleanup + CLAUDE.md correction. description: Deferred deeper alternative to the I4 m≥3 fix (FINDINGS §C11). aic_corner_extract returns a FROBENIUS-orthonormal {C_l} corner basis; for dim S_{P,Q}>=2 the lem_PQ_Hilb Euclidean Gram G != I by O(1) (||G-I||~0.14, ||C_0||_op~0.925 on mixconj(6,3)). Deeper fix: have aic_corner_extract (or a variant) return an OPERATOR-orthonormal basis so G=I, the plain conjugate-transpose IS the true involution, and that caller can use a tight unit_tol again (and §F-I4-2's norm-equivalence claim holds by construction). Not needed for correctness; restores the involution invariant's meaning at the caller. See src/aic_cstar_extension.c, paper/FINDINGS.md §C11, docs/research/lem_extension_spec.md §F-I4-2.

title: I5 oblique-wrapper aic_sgn convergence wall (n>=6/m>=3) + eta>0 multi-class coverage gap
description: Both independent I5 implementations hit aic_sgn_newton_schulz non-convergence on near-boundary corner matrices in Stage-2 oblique-wrapper lem_extension for ambient n>=6 or block m>=3 (prec-independent -> a convergence-BASIN issue, not precision; FINDINGS §C11). So aic_cstar_build's eta>0 oblique tests are scoped to in-basin n in {4,5}, m=2; the clean dim-independence proof is the eta=0 sweep (flat constant=0 to dim_A=20). CONSEQUENCE: the Stage-3 multi-class merge + the errreduce_unit running-P_total!=1_n branch run only at eta=0 (machine-zero). Diagnose: aic_sgn basin-coverage gap (fix: wider-basin sgn = aic-68c scaled-Newton/Kenney-Laub, or an eig-based fallback for the corner matrices) vs the §D1/aic-3qv structural gap-degeneracy stop condition. Fixing it unblocks an in-basin eta>0 >=2-class fixture to exercise the coverage gap.
notes: aic_sgn CONVERGENCE WALL FIXED (commit pending): midpoint-iterate + a-posteriori sign certificate; aic_cstar_build now completes for n<=6 m=2 oblique (mixconj(6,2) iso_def/eta=2.57). Diagnosis was classification (D) (termination/precision-floor vs inherited radius), NOT out-of-basin (A)/dispatch (B)/structural-§D1 (C). RESIDUAL on this bead: (1) m>=3 now gets PAST sgn but hits a DISTINCT aic_dhom_approx involution-symmetry tol (relates to aic-92i, the errreduce_unit unit_tol); (2) the eta>0 MULTI-CLASS merge coverage gap still needs an in-basin >=2-equivalence-class oblique fixture. FINDINGS §C11.
close_reason: RESOLVED. The aic_sgn convergence wall was fixed first (commit f27aea7, midpoint+certificate). The downstream m>=3 abort was then diagnosed (classification C, NOT a §D1 stop condition): aic_dhom_approx's involution-symmetry assert measured the PLAIN conjugate-transpose defect, an O(1) artifact of the Ha codomain's lem_PQ_Hilb Gram G!=I (the corner basis is Frobenius-ON not operator-ON, ||C_0||_op=0.925) — NOT a *-linearity break (the true G-twisted adjoint is exact to 1e-16); plus a separate eps_target=0 mis-target (h11v is only an O(delta+eps)-hom, .tex:1157). Fixed at the extension.c lem_approx caller: eps_target=2*aic_ecstar_defect_assoc(A_parent)=O(eta), unit_tol=2.0. mixconj(6,3) now completes (iso/eta=0.43, bijective); the universality canary EXTENDS to m>=3 (c-ratio 2.05, dimension-independent); and the eta>0 MULTI-CLASS coverage gap is CLOSED (T4: a block-structured oblique fixture yields 2 classes, exercising Stage-3 cor_merge_sum + errreduce_unit's running-P_total!=1_n branch at eta>0; num_blocks=2, bijective, iso/eta~0.004). test_cstar_build n=101, full make test green. Deeper fix (operator-ON corner basis -> G=I -> recover the tight involution tol) = bead aic-5aq.

title: aic_mat_opnorm Gram path false-fails on near-zero off-diagonal entries with matmul-accumulated radius
description: aic_mat_opnorm forms the Gram M^dag M and routes through aic_mat_herm_max_eig -> aic_mat_int_assert_hermitian, whose relative+absolute-floor Hermiticity tol (bead aic-2yo) STILL false-fails when an off-diagonal Gram entry has midpoint ~0 but a matmul-ACCUMULATED arb radius exceeding the absolute floor 2^-(prec-8) -- even though the input matrix is tight (radius ~1e-72) and genuinely Hermitian to ~1e-20. Hit by: corner lem_alpha (gamma, eta=0 orthogonal columns), corner Ha-map (G-I, dim>=2 eta>0 -- was a BLOCKER SIGABRT on a valid in-hypothesis input), corner lem_PQR (cdot output X.Y with ~1e-70 radius despite ||X.Y||~1). WORKAROUND in place: aic_corner_gamma_opnorm_ub computes ||mid(M)||_op + ||rad(M)||_F (certified upper bound) sidestepping the Gram path. ROOT-CAUSE FIX options: (a) symmetrize the Gram (G+G^dag)/2 before the Hermiticity check; (b) make the Hermiticity tol account for accumulated radius (radius-relative, not just magnitude-relative); (c) an eig-free opnorm route (power iteration on A^dag A, bead aic-w4o.3). Until fixed, new modules must use the mid+radius helper for op-norms of near-orthonormal / near-zero-offdiag Grams. Neighbour of aic-w4o.1.
close_reason: Fixed: aic_mat_gram now splits the Gram into an exact-Hermitian midpoint Gmid (zero radius, passes the Hermiticity predicate for the right reason) + a rigorous Weyl perturbation bound R=||G-Gmid||_F; opnorm/singular_values run the certified eig on Gmid then inflate by R (clamp PSD). My planned symmetrize-only fix was insufficient (midpoints already conjugate; the false-fail is radius-only). Hostile-reviewed (sampling-proved rigorous: 0 enclosure failures over 7500 interior points; values byte-identical on tight inputs; fail-closed basin preserved; herm_max_eig teeth intact). Unblocks the oblique S_P-wrapper corner path (FINDINGS §C5/§C10 RESOLVED) for I5. aic_corner_gamma_opnorm_ub workaround now retireable (separate cleanup).

title: aic_mat assert_hermitian: absolute tol false-fails on graded/ill-conditioned Gram (singular_values/opnorm abort)
description: SURFACED BY aic-dbo.2 gen5 (graded diagonal, nla 5c). aic_mat_int_assert_hermitian (src/aic_mat_spectral.c:58-76) uses an ABSOLUTE tol 2^-(prec-8) and checks |H_ij - conj(H_ji)| <= tol. For a Gram H=D^dag D of a graded diagonal D=diag(r^0..r^{n-1}), the diagonal H_ii=r^{2i} carries an imaginary-part arb radius ~r^{2i}*2^-prec (from arb_pow/arb_mul); the check |H_ii-conj(H_ii)| = 2*Im-radius ~ 2*r^{2i}*2^-prec vs tol=256*2^-prec FALSE-FAILS once r^{2i} > 128 -- a PRECISION-INDEPENDENT abort (raising prec scales radius and tol together; verified still aborts at prec=1024). So aic_mat_singular_values and aic_mat_opnorm ABORT on any sufficiently ill-conditioned/graded Hermitian input (measured: aborts for range >= ~5 at n=6). Rule 3 (all bugs deep): the Hermiticity guard tol must be RELATIVE / magnitude-scaled (e.g. tol*max(|H_ij|,|H_ji|) or scale by ||H||_F or compare midpoint symmetry instead of self-subtracting two copies of the same ball). gen5's self-test works around it via a Gram-free certified diagonal SV read (SVs of a positive diagonal ARE its diagonal). Fix unblocks opnorm/SVD on the ill-conditioned adversarial family (nla Family 5) and any graded Gram downstream.
close_reason: FIXED. aic_mat_int_assert_hermitian now uses a RELATIVE + absolute-floor tol tol*(1+|H_ij|+|H_ji|) instead of the bare absolute 2^-(prec-8). The flagged asymmetry on a faithfully-rounded Hermitian was the arb ball RADIUS (which grows with entry magnitude), so a Gram D^dag D of a graded/ill-conditioned D false-failed once an entry exceeded ~128, precision-independently -> aic_mat_opnorm/singular_values aborted for cond >~ 1e2. Refactored into a non-aborting predicate aic_mat_int_is_hermitian (so the teeth are testable); assert wraps it. test_mat test7 (n=29): graded Gram kappa=1e8 now accepted, opnorm(graded_diag)=1.0000e8 exactly (was abort); GENUINE non-Hermitian still rejected ([[1,2],[3,1]], [[1,i],[i,1]]); real symmetric accepted. Mutation-proven (revert to absolute tol -> graded Gram rejected -> RED at test_mat.c:524). Full make test green (no regression in the foundational routine used by eig_hermitian/herm_max_eig/opnorm/singular_values). Surfaced by aic-dbo.2 gen5; closes the corpus-finding loop. Unblocks opnorm/SVD on the nla Family 5 ill-conditioned instances.

title: funcalc: globally-convergent matrix-sign for NON-NORMAL superoperators (relax Frobenius basin to spectral/no-imaginary-axis precondition)
description: assoc_ecsa (aic-92f) applies theta(2Phi-1)=prop_P to the n^2 x n^2 SUPEROPERATOR S_Phi, which is NON-NORMAL (Phi HP-preserving != HS-self-adjoint). funcalc's aic_sgn_newton_schulz AND aic_sgn_denman_beavers both ASSERT the FROBENIUS-norm basin ||I-X^2||_F<1 (src/aic_funcalc_sgn.c:87,97,147). For S=2S_Phi-1 this is 4||S_Phi^2-S_Phi||_F<1, which (orchestrator bound) is <= 4 sqrt(n) eta so it is DIMENSION-DEPENDENT and STRICTER than the paper's eta<1/4; it TRIPS near eta->1/4, large n, or high departure-from-normality, even though sgn(S) is mathematically well-defined (rho(S_Phi^2-S_Phi)<=eta => eigenvalues of S off the imaginary axis). Higham Functions of Matrices Thm 5.6 (web research leg): Newton/Denman-Beavers iteration X_{k+1}=1/2(X_k+X_k^{-1}) converges GLOBALLY for ANY matrix with no purely-imaginary eigenvalues -- NOT basin-limited. TASK: add a funcalc sgn variant (audition: scaled-Newton w/ determinantal scaling (Kenney-Laub 1992), Denman-Beavers coupled form, and Nakatsukasa-Freund Zolotarev 2-iter SIAM Rev 2016 as candidates) whose precondition is the SPECTRAL no-imaginary-axis condition (certify via arb eigenvalue enclosure / a-posteriori ||Y^2-I|| residual ball), so the FULL eta<1/4 non-normal regime is reachable. Cross-check vs the in-basin Newton-Schulz where both apply. Refs: Higham 2008 Ch5; Kenney-Laub SIMAX 1992; Bai-Demmel SIMAX 1998 (non-normal stability); Nakatsukasa-Freund SIAM Rev 2016. Blocks the near-boundary/high-non-normality regime of aic-92f I1 (small-eta regime works without it).
close_reason: LANDED (orchestrated research->implement->Mathematica oracle->hostile review->fix). funcalc now has a globally-convergent non-normal sgn reaching the full spectral rho(I-X^2)<1 regime, eig-free (no aic-w4o.1 dep). Route: Newton 1/2(Y+Y^-1) (Higham Functions of Matrices Thm 5.6, global+quadratic) gated by a Gelfand spectral-radius precondition ||M^k||_F^{1/k}<1 on M=I-X^2 (k=1 is the old op/Frobenius check; non-normal needs a few powers; k_max=32, fail loud), plus an a-posteriori ||Y^2-I||+||XY-YX|| certificate (genuinely fires at prec=53). aic_sgn auto-dispatches (in-basin Newton-Schulz byte-for-byte, else global Newton); aic_prop_P relaxed to the spectral rho(P^2-P)<1/4 so theta/prop_P/aic_assoc_regularize reach the full NON-NORMAL eta<1/4 regime. New src/aic_funcalc_global.c (200 LOC), tests/test_funcalc_global.c (n=12). Teeth: 2x2 closed-form oracle sgn([[1.05,20],[0,-0.95]])=[[1,20],[0,-1]] (op-norm 2.005 out of basin, rho 0.132 cert k=2; Mathematica-confirmed 2 ways to 58 digits); assoc payoff test_assoc2 U6 (oblique compress_idemp(4,1)xdephasing, ||S^2-S||_op=0.42>=1/4 OLD aborts, rho=0.21 cert 4rho<1 k=6, regularize gives S_tilde^2=S_tilde to 8.9e-70) with a UNITALITY sign witness Phi_tilde(1)=1 (||S_tilde vec(I)-vec(I)||=4e-70 vs sqrt(n)=2 for -sgn), mutation-proven. Hostile review: NO blockers; 3 non-blocking findings (docstring overstated generality; stale regularize comment; sign-blind U6 assertion) ALL fixed inline + the sign witness mutation-proven. Full suite green (16 binaries, zero warnings); ALGORITHM.md + docs/research/sgn_global_research.md added. Scaled-Newton (Kenney-Laub) kept as a deferred Pareto candidate -> aic-8hz-followup bead.

title: ecstar HOPM: Pi_A subspace-polar accept-guard teeth on a non-polar-closed A
description: Cycle-2 finding: the Pi_A(polar(C)) projection in the HOPM block update keeps witnesses in A, but EVERY Cycle-2 test instance has A=Img(exact Phi), a GENUINE C* algebra (polar/von-Neumann-closed), so polar(C) already lands in A and a skip-Pi_A mutation leaves test_hopm_soundness GREEN (verified). The Pi_A CORRECTNESS contribution can only be exercised by a genuine eta>0 A=Img Phi~ (Phi~=theta(2Phi-1)) where the associator is also nonzero so candidates get ACCEPTED -- i.e. once aic-92f (assoc_ecsa) lands. Add a soundness teeth then: build A=Img Phi~ for an eta>0 channel, run assoc HOPM, assert witness in A, and mutation-prove skip-Pi_A makes a witness leave A (proj_residual O(eta)). The accept-guard's monotone-ascent half is ALREADY mutation-proven (never-iterate -> canary/searches RED).
close_reason: DELIVERED by aic-92f Increment 2, test_assoc2 U4 (test_u4). On the genuine eta>0 A=Img(Phi_tilde) (dep(3)+conj(dep(3)), non-polar-closed because eps-C* not von-Neumann-closed), the assoc HOPM witness is IN A (aic_ecstar_proj_residual < 5.9e-16, a CERTIFIED arb opnorm of X-Pi_A X) and lo<=ratio@witness; ||polar(wX)-Pi_A polar(wX)||=1.86e-2>0 proves A is genuinely non-polar-closed so Pi_A does real work. Hostile review MUTATION-PROVED the Pi_A CORRECTNESS half: disabling the Pi_A projection in aic_ehk_block_candidate makes U4's proj_residual fire RED (witness residuals 2.5e-2/2.2e-2/5.4e-2 >> 1e-9 tol). The monotone-ascent half was already proven (never-iterate->RED). This is exactly the bead's ask (build A=Img Phi~ eta>0, run assoc HOPM, assert witness in A, mutation-prove skip-Pi_A leaves A).

title: Real O(eta) eps-sweep validation of ecstar defect estimators across almost-idempotent channels
description: ecstar (aic-knm) ships with the eta=0 oracle (machine-zero) + a synthetic star-perturbation canary. The REAL validation -- feed A=ImgPhi_tilde for a one-parameter family of genuinely eta-idempotent UCP maps (e.g. depolarizing Phi_t=(1-t)id+t Dep_d, eta(t) known analytically; paper example tex:367 eta sqrt(1-eta)), confirm every multiplicative defect grows = O(eta) AND the defect/eta ratio does NOT grow with dim A (universality canary tex:484) -- needs Phi_tilde=theta(2Phi-1) on the SUPEROPERATOR 2Phi-1, which is assoc_ecsa's job. DEPENDS ON aic-92f (assoc_ecsa). eta measurable via eta_idempotence (Julia+MOSEK) and certifiable via aic_cbnorm_certify.
notes: SUBSTANTIALLY DELIVERED by aic-92f Increment 2 (test_assoc2 U2+U3) with an OP-NORM eta-PROXY (||S_Phi^2-S_Phi||_op), NOT yet the certified cb-norm eta. U2: real eta>0 family dep(d)+conj(dep(d)), assoc & cstar defects grow O(proxy), submult ~0, involution exact, all vanishing at t->0 + nonzero teeth (cstar mutation-proven). U3: dimension-sweep canary assoc/proxy FLAT (spread 1.42 over dim_A=4..16, threshold 2.0), mutation-proven to catch linear AND sqrt(dim) injection. REMAINING (why not closed): the bead specifies eta measurable via eta_idempotence (Julia+MOSEK) / certifiable via aic_cbnorm_certify; the op-proxy could relate to the true cb-eta by a dimension-DEPENDENT factor, so 'defect/proxy flat' does not yet RIGOROUSLY imply 'defect/cb-eta flat' (the paper's actual universality statement). Refinement: re-run U3 normalizing by the certified cb-eta to close the proxy<->cb-eta scaling gap. Low effort given the existing certify pipeline.

title: Julia end-to-end certified-bracket wrapper certify(kraus)->(lo,hi)
description: Tie the aic-m24 tight certifier into the Julia package end-to-end: a certify(kraus)->(lo,hi) in AlmostIdempotentChannels.jl that solves the MAX primal (diamond_norm_watrous_primal) + MIN dual (diamond_norm_dual) — both already in src/sdp.jl — to get the feasible points (X,P,Q),(Y0,Y1), builds Choi via the shim, ccalls aic_cbnorm_certify_d (exported in libaic.so), and returns the rigorous arb bracket. Test end-to-end on live channels (GADC) and cross-check the bracket contains the wolframscript-oracle / MOSEK value. Integration polish (the C certifier + value entry point are done in aic-m24); ties to E5 Julia package wiring (aic-obc).

title: wolframscript independent diamond-norm oracle for eta=||Phi^2-Phi||_cb
description: Third independent cross-check rung for the eta-idempotence golden master (after MOSEK + CLARABEL). A wolframscript (.wls) tool implementing the Watrous 2012 diamond-norm SDP in Mathematica's complex-native SemidefiniteOptimization (verified syntax: VectorGreaterEqual[{M,0},{SemidefiniteCone,n}]; the bare M>=0 op-form is an entrywise TRAP), (2/n)-normalized for Convention-A Choi. Validates against the exact analytic anchors and cross-checks the committed eta_ref in tests/fixtures_d24.inc.h. Also establishes the high-precision (WorkingPrecision) oracle to validate the certified-arb tight ball in aic-m24 increment 3.
close_reason: Delivered tools/diamond_oracle.wls: independent Mathematica (14.3.0, SemidefiniteOptimization, complex-native) implementation of the Watrous 2012 diamond-norm SDP, (2/n)-normalized. Reproduces all exact analytic anchors <1e-6 and CONFIRMS the MOSEK golden master to max |diff|=1.093e-7 over the 17 d24 fixtures (worst at complex_qubit; eta=0 oracles exact). Third independent solver rung (Mathematica IPM vs MOSEK vs CLARABEL). Re-run by orchestrator (clean exit 0), numbers reproduced. description: Follow-on to aic-d24 (increment 1 delivered the C Choi-diff substrate + the Julia+MOSEK golden master computing the diamond-norm VALUE). (1) CERTIFIED-ARB cb-ball: aic_cbnorm_certify (incr 3b) — tight rigorous two-sided ball on eta=||Phi^2-Phi||_cb from two MOSEK feasible points (MAX primal + MIN dual, solved separately in Julia, incr 3a) via arb feasibility-restoration: LOWER convex-combination toward the Slater center, UPPER eigenvalue-shift, all PSD tests one-sided herm_max_eig (no full eig, dodges aic-w4o.1), dispatch+fail-loud with eig-free fallback. Widths ~6e-14..6e-12 (~10^12x tighter than eig-free). Hostile-reviewed (no blockers); ALL load-bearing choices mutation-proven (partial-trace direction via the asymmetric paper_0p1 fixture; (2/n)/eps*n/t corrections via test_certify family G; psd_defect-ubound + E-symmetrization via test_certify_teeth GAP-ii/iv) and re-verified RED by the orchestrator. (2) REUSABLE VALUE ENTRY POINT: eta_idempotence in AlmostIdempotentChannels.jl (incr 2b) ccalls the C shim (incr 2a, libaic.so) + solves the Watrous SDP via Convex+MOSEK. Plus the always-valid eig-free bracket [||J||_F/n, 2||J||_F] (incr 1) and an INDEPENDENT wolframscript diamond-norm oracle (aic-cne) confirming the MOSEK golden master to 1.1e-7. The Julia end-to-end certify(kraus)->(lo,hi) wrapper is split to a follow-up (E5/aic-obc).

title: Artin-Wedderburn block decomposition of A=ImgPhi (prop_Gamma explicit form, Delta/Gamma Kraus reps)
description: th_idemp_structure's explicit prop_Gamma form (tex:2106, Gamma_j(X)=Tr_{E_j}(W_j X W_j^dag (1_{L_j}(x)gamma_j))) and the Kraus reps of Delta/Gamma (needed by factorize) require the Artin-Wedderburn / Wedderburn decomposition of the C*-algebra A=ImgPhi into +_j M_{n_j} (x) 1_{m_j} on the carrier M=+_j L_j(x)E_j. NOT needed for the eta=0 oracle (the Lambda-factorization Gamma=(Delta^dag Delta)^-1 Delta^dag Lambda satisfies all th_idemp_structure RELATIONS without it) -> split out from aic-wuh.
design: Candidate slate (web research 2026-05-28): (1) commutant A'={K_a,K_a^dag}' (Wolf Thm 6.12/Lemma 6.4, mediatum.ub.tum.de/doc/1701036) + eigendecomposition of a RANDOM Hermitian element of A' -> minimal central projections / isotypic blocks (HJPW03 Thm10 quant-ph/0402056; Choi-Kribs PRL96 050501 quant-ph/0507213). (2) Murota-Kanno-Kojima-Kojima 2010 + Maehara-Murota 2011 error-controlled matrix-*-algebra block-diagonalization. (3) Blume-Kohout-Ng-Poulin-Viola IPS (arXiv:0705.4282, 1006.1358), poly(n^2). HARD SPOT: equal block sizes => degenerate eig cluster -> 2+ random draws or Murota tolerance; certified version ties to aic-w4o.1. Golden master: Mathematica exact Wedderburn (needs VPN) or Murota MATLAB code (optimization-online.org/2009/05/2292). Audition on: robustness to equal/multiplicity blocks, accuracy@prec, certifiability, wall time.
acceptance_criteria: Given Phi idempotent UCP, recovers block sizes (n_j,m_j) and unitary W matching KNOWN structure on the aic-wuh test channels (block-diag n1=2,n2=3; noiseless subsystem L(x)E; dephasing commutative); reconstructs Delta,Gamma as aic_ucp_kraus with ||Gamma w - 1_A||=0; cross-checked vs an independent oracle (Mathematica/Murota).

title: Extend bench harness: multi-dimensional perf metrics + Pareto-domination reporting
description: Per the Pareto-audition rule (CLAUDE.md Law 4, memory feedback-pareto-audition): the harness must emit several perf columns per candidate so Pareto analysis is possible, not just one ns/op. Add: wall time (have), iteration count, peak memory, accuracy / certified-ball tightness at fixed prec, and robustness on adversarial inputs (ties to epic aic-dbo). Provide a small Pareto-frontier reporter that flags domination / non-domination across candidates and regimes. Motivating case: the contraction Picard-vs-Anderson tie (aic-n3n).

title: Near-boundary / fail-loud guard stress tests (precondition edges)
description: Push each precondition right to the edge and just over it: ||X^2-I|| -> 1^- and the sub-ULP straddle of =1 (nla 7a); prop_P delta -> 1/4^- (nla 7b); contraction c -> 1^- (nla 7c); eta -> 1/4^- (domain 2A). Assert the arb guard ABORTS (fail-loud) when the certified ball straddles the boundary -- exactly the case where double arithmetic is unconditionally wrong -- and proceeds with a certified result strictly inside. The sgn domain guard (the fragility chokepoint per the research) gets the hardest stress.

title: Policy+retrofit: every module's tests/benches draw adversarial instances (bound-holds OR fail-loud)
description: Make it a standing rule and retrofit existing modules (mat, funcalc, contraction, ...): each module's test/bench must exercise the relevant adversarial families and assert one of two acceptable outcomes on each evil input -- the certified bound provably holds, OR the routine fails loud (assert/refuse). 'Passes on nice inputs' counts as not yet tested.

title: Shared, parameterized benchmark-instance corpus (representative regimes)
description: Current benchmarks use ad-hoc per-module instances (small n<=16, deterministic, single-instance; mat eig even uses a deliberately well-SEPARATED spectrum = the easy case, not the project's real degenerate projections). For Law-4 performance auditioning to be meaningful and comparable across modules, build a shared instance generator (e.g. include/aic_instances.h or bench helpers) parameterized by the regimes that actually matter in arXiv:2405.02434: (a) eta-idempotent UCP maps at varying eta and dim (Choi/Kraus); (b) eps-C* algebras / near-projections at varying eps; (c) DEGENERATE vs non-degenerate Hermitian spectra and block-multiplicity structure (direct sums of B(L_j)); (d) a dim sweep (n up to where arb certified ops stay tractable) with FIXED random seeds for reproducibility. Retrofit existing benches (mat/funcalc) to draw from it. Make perf numbers comparable and tied to real workloads, not toy inputs.
notes: Unify with adversarial generators aic-dbo.2 (epic aic-dbo); corpus = representative + adversarial instances from docs/adversarial/.

title: [env] Laptop clock skew -> make 'Clock skew detected' warnings + Ninja 'manifest dirty' failures
description: On this laptop the OS clock (date) reads BEHIND the mtimes of working-tree files (observed: OS 23:39 while CLAUDE.md/HANDOFF.md/several tests are at 23:46, ~7 min in the future). make/gmake emit repeated 'Clock skew detected. Your build may be incomplete.' warnings — NON-fatal (builds exit 0, artifacts present, ctest -L fast green). Ninja HARD-fails with 'manifest dirty after 100 tries' under the same skew, which is WHY the Makefile wrapper + dev builds use the cmake DEFAULT (Unix Makefiles) generator instead of Ninja. Root cause is the host clock (NTP/VM), not the build system; a real fix needs host NTP sync (sudo, out of agent scope). Mitigations in place: default Make generator (BUILDING.md Gotchas); 'rm -rf build' clears a stuck Ninja build dir. Surfaced during aic-95g.1.

title: Packaging: flip to -fvisibility=hidden + curated AIC_EXPORT ABI surface (coordinate with umbrella aic-7xx)
description: Inc2 of the CMake migration generated the AIC_EXPORT macro infra (build/include/aic/aic_export.h) but Inc4 kept DEFAULT symbol visibility on purpose. Reason: the install step exposes ALL include/aic/*.h module headers as public, and the project has not curated a public ABI surface (aic.h's docstring explicitly defers declaring the full surface, Law 4). Flipping to -fvisibility=hidden now would either export nothing (breaking the Julia ccall shim symbols) or require premature ABI curation, and would make the shared-lib ABI inconsistent with the installed-as-public headers. WHEN the public surface is deliberately defined (coordinate with aic-7xx umbrella aic_all.h): set CMAKE_C_VISIBILITY_PRESET hidden + CMAKE_VISIBILITY_INLINES_HIDDEN, annotate the public/ccall surface (the three *_shim.h + the umbrella API + aic_version) with AIC_EXPORT, and verify with nm -D that the .so exports exactly the curated ABI. Tests already link the STATIC lib so they keep full internal-symbol access. NOTE: default visibility now does NOT block the Julia JLL (aic-95g.2) — ccall symbols stay exported.

title: errreduce_unit: unit_tol=2.0 over-relaxes aic_dhom_approx involution-symmetry assert
description: aic_cstar_errreduce_unit (src/aic_cstar_classes.c) passes unit_tol=2.0 to aic_dhom_approx to stop the legitimate ||v(I_B)-1_n||~1 wrapper drift tripping the BLOWUP guard. But that same param ALSO gates the involution-symmetry assert (aic_dhom_approx.c), so a genuine involution break in [eps,2.0] is not caught HERE (the Newton contraction + max_steps guards DO still catch divergence; hostile review finding #2). Refine: decouple the unit-blowup ceiling from the involution tol in aic_dhom_approx, OR check involution separately in errreduce_unit. Non-blocking (FINDINGS §C11).
notes: EXPLAINED + practically resolved. The diagnosis (aic-1vp) showed the errreduce_unit/extension unit_tol relaxation is JUSTIFIED, not a defect: the involution-symmetry defect on the Ha-twisted codomain (lem_PQ_Hilb Gram G!=I for dim S_{P,Q}>=2) is an O(1) STRUCTURAL artifact of the Frobenius-ON (not operator-ON) corner basis, NOT a real *-linearity break (the true G-twisted adjoint G^{-1}(.)^H G is exact to 1e-16). So relaxing unit_tol at these Ha-codomain callers is correct; the assert stays tight for genuine-codomain callers. The clean fix that would RECOVER the tight tol everywhere is aic-5aq (operator-ON corner basis -> G=I). This bead is thus superseded by aic-5aq; keeping open as a pointer.

title: funcalc: scaled-Newton (Kenney-Laub) sgn variant as a Pareto candidate near eta->1/4

NOT built because our X=2S_Phi-1 spectrum starts near +/-1, so unscaled converges in ~7 iters (measured). AUDITION TRIGGER: a future channel near eta->1/4 / large n where the unscaled global Newton iteration count climbs (cap is 100, fail-loud) OR the Gelfand k climbs toward k_max=32. Then audition scaled-Newton vs unscaled on iteration count / arb wall-time / robustness, keep the Pareto frontier. Refs: Higham Functions of Matrices Ch.5; Kenney-Laub SIMAX 1992; Bai-Demmel SIMAX 1998 (non-normal cond ~1/sep, sep>=2sqrt(1-4eta)).

(2) add a direct double-vs-arb single-evaluation cross-check of the HOPM double kernel (aic_ecstar_hopm.c has no in-loop double-vs-arb check; evaluate one objval/associator in double and in arb, assert agreement ~1e-12) -- guards a silent wrong-objective kernel bug. (3) tighten the universality canary: the hmax/hmin<4.0 threshold over dim_A 8->45 catches growth ~dim^p for p>0.8 (the d^{3/2} trap fires, max/min=17.3 verified), but milder sub-linear (d^{0.5}) inflation passes; widen the dim range or lower the threshold.

title: ecstar HOPM: Phi-dagger adjoint gradient (drop the per-k basis loop)
description: Cycle-2 speedup. The HOPM block update computes the A-gradient C=sum_k <u,h(B_k,..)v> B_k by LOOPING over the d basis elements, each recomputing the full associator (4 stars x r-Kraus Phi-applies): O(d*r*n^3) per block update. Since c_k=<u,h(B_k,Y,Z)v> is LINEAR in B_k, C can be assembled in ONE shot via the HS-adjoint Phi^dag (=Phi* Schrodinger form sum K X K^dag) without the k-loop: C = (gradient of Re<u, Phi(Phi(XY)Z)-Phi(XPhi(YZ)) v> wrt X). This is the main speedup for larger n,d (the d=9 dim_A=45 HOPM is ~0.9s now; the Cycle-1 basis sweep at dim_A=45 is ~190s and is the real ceiling, also worth an adjoint/early-exit pass). DEFER carefully: adjoint bookkeeping is exactly where a sign/transpose bug hides (Rule 3); cross-check the adjoint gradient against the current per-k gradient to machine precision before switching.

title: Certified SDP UPPER bound for eps-C* axiom defects (Watrous bilinear exact + SOS/Lasserre trilinear)
description: The certified-UPPER-bound rung for the ecstar (aic-knm) axiom-defect estimators. Submultiplicativity (ax_prodnorm) and C*-identity (ax_C*) defects are BILINEAR/quadratic operator-norm sups -> exact via a Watrous-style SDP (arXiv:0901.4709/1207.5726), reuse the Julia+MOSEK infra (src/sdp.jl, eta_idempotence pipeline). Associativity (ax_assoc) is a TRILINEAR injective-tensor-norm sup -> rigorous upper bound needs a level-r SOS/Lasserre hierarchy (arXiv:2310.17827, 2412.13191); ESCALATION: certifying the upper bound in arb from a MOSEK feasible point follows the aic_cbnorm_certify pattern. Avoid the O(d^1.5) Frobenius/dim trap (would falsely trip the universality canary). Lower bound (HOPM) vs SDP upper bound agreement is the key cross-check. Blocked-by ecstar cycle 1+2.

BOTH splits. Add a test channel whose Img-Phi operators are block-diagonal in only ONE of the {M | M^perp} splits, so the block-diag assertion alone catches a wrong carrier. A genuine rung-2 cross-check of the extracted subspaces (carrier M, algebra A) needs a certified-arb degenerate eig/SVD oracle == bead aic-w4o.1; until then the subspaces are validated only via the relation defects (which DO catch a wrong carrier via GCD/DGC, per probe8). Wire the rung-2 subspace cross-check when aic-w4o.1 lands.
acceptance_criteria: A single-split block-diagonal test channel exists for which aic_idemp_defect_blockdiag goes nonzero under a carrier->M^perp swap (mutation-proven). When aic-w4o.1 lands, test_idemp gains a genuine double(LAPACK)-vs-arb(certified) comparison of Pi_M and Pi_A.
